name: beautifulsoup4
summary: HTML and XML parsing library for extracting data from web pages with a simple Pythonic API.

story.problem: You've fetched HTML from a web page with requests. Now you're staring at a wall of tags, trying to extract product prices, article text, or table rows with regex. Regex breaks the moment the site adds a CSS class. You need a proper HTML tree parser.
story.mental_model: BeautifulSoup turns an HTML string into a traversable tree. Every tag becomes an object with .text, .attrs, and child/parent navigation. You find elements by tag name, CSS class, id, or attribute — like jQuery selectors but in Python.
story.when_to_use: Scraping static HTML pages, extracting data from email templates, parsing HTML in tests, processing XML feeds, cleaning up messy HTML.
story.when_not_to_use: JavaScript-rendered pages (use Playwright or Selenium). High-volume scraping at scale (use Scrapy). When the site has a proper API — always prefer the API.

alternatives.name: lxml
alternatives.reason: Much faster for large documents. BS4 can use lxml as its parser backend.

alternatives.name: parsel
alternatives.reason: Scrapy's selector library — XPath and CSS selectors, faster than BS4.

alternatives.name: playwright
alternatives.reason: Needed for JS-rendered pages; can extract from the live DOM.

nodes.label: BeautifulSoup(html, parser)
nodes.summary: Main class. `soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')`. Parsers: html.parser, lxml, html5lib.

nodes.label: .find_all(tag, attrs)
nodes.summary: Find all matching elements. Returns a list. Accepts tag, attrs dict, CSS class, id, string.

nodes.label: .select(css_selector)
nodes.summary: CSS selector syntax: `soup.select('table.data tr td')`. Returns list like find_all.

nodes.label: NavigableString
nodes.summary: A text node inside a tag. Accessed via tag.string or iterating tag.children.

tasks.title: Scrape article titles from a news page

steps.label: Fetch
steps.code:
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

html = requests.get('https://news.ycombinator.com').text
soup = BeautifulSoup(html, 'html.parser')

steps.label: Extract

tasks.title: Parse a table into a list of dicts

steps.label: Extract rows
steps.code:
data = []
for row in rows[1:]:
    cells = [td.text.strip() for td in row.find_all('td')]
    data.append(dict(zip(headers, cells)))
